summary: Frontend testing completed for Django car damage assessment app. Basic page rendering works correctly, but core authentication flow is blocked due to CSRF configuration issues.

critical_bugs.issue: CSRF token validation fails on phone form submission returning 403 Forbidden
critical_bugs.impact: Blocks entire user authentication flow - users cannot proceed past phone input
critical_bugs.details: Form contains valid CSRF token but Django still rejects POST requests with 403 error

critical_bugs.issue: Protected pages return 200 instead of redirecting to login (302)
critical_bugs.impact: Authentication middleware not working - security vulnerability
critical_bugs.details: login_required_custom decorator not properly redirecting unauthenticated users

configuration_issues.issue: CSRF verification failing for external domain requests
configuration_issues.suggested_fix: Check CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS, ALLOWED_HOSTS, or CSRF middleware configuration

integration_issues.issue: Form submission blocked by CSRF 403 error

passed_tests: Phone input page loads correctly with proper styling and layout
Phone input field accepts formatted phone numbers (+7 format)
All required UI elements present with correct data-testids
Contacts page loads with phone (+7 495 123-45-67) and address information
Mobile-adapted red-white minimalist design renders correctly
Bottom navigation elements present and styled correctly
Protected pages redirect logic implemented (though not working)
CSRF tokens generated and embedded in forms correctly

success_percentage.backend: 42% (3/7 tests passed - page loads work, form submission fails)
success_percentage.frontend: 70% (UI renders correctly but main workflow blocked)

action_item_for_main_agent: CRITICAL: Fix CSRF configuration issues preventing form submissions. Check CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS, ALLOWED_HOSTS settings, and verify CSRF middleware configuration for external domain. Also fix authentication middleware (login_required_custom decorator) not properly redirecting unauthorized users.
